reasoning: RULE A applied: get_japan_heritage returns 日本遺産 stories — a different designation program from 重伝建地区 (Preservation Districts for Groups of Traditional Buildings). The response body is almost entirely a browse_advisory pivot guide with canonical_tool suggestions, not actual traditional architecture site data. Per RULE A, the data returned is scored as-is: it equips the agent only with routing hints (e.g. search_area q=合掌造り), not the specific 重伝建 districts the query targets. Single root cause (wrong-tool / retrieval miss) per RULE B drives intent, practical_usefulness, and specificity down; other dims are scored on what was actually returned (well-formed, grounded, accurate advisory).
missing_data: Actual 重要伝統的建造物群保存地区 (重伝建地区) entries — named districts such as Tsumago-juku, Shirakawa-go, Gion Higashiyama, Ouchi-juku, Takayama Sanmachi, etc. — with addresses, descriptions, and access info.
improvement_hint: Add 重伝建地区 (文化庁 national designation, ~130 districts) as a dedicated data source and tool (e.g. get_traditional_districts) or route this intent from get_japan_heritage browse_advisory to search_area q=重要伝統的建造物群保存地区. The current advisory block lists gassho-zukuri farmhouses but omits the broader 重伝建 category entirely.
